finding.contract_claim: L25-25: `Path` is imported from `pathlib` (L25) but is never directly used anywhere in the file. All path construction uses `config.root_path / ...` where `config.root_path` is already a `Path` object — the `Path` constructor itself is never called.
finding.observed_behavior: Remove `Path` from the import: change `from pathlib import Path` to remove that name, or delete the import entirely if no other pathlib names are needed.
finding.triage_reasoning:
The grep returns exactly one hit for `Path` in the file — the import on L25. Every path constructed in the file uses the `/` operator on `config.root_path` (which is already a `Path` instance injected by `Config`); the `Path` constructor is never invoked directly. No other pathlib name is imported alongside it, so the entire `from pathlib import Path` line is dead.

Reality ✓ — The symbol is imported but has zero call-sites in the file.
Significance ✓ — Dead imports are noise, confuse readers, and may trip linters/audits.
Actionability ✓ — Delete `from pathlib import Path` (the whole line; it's the sole import from pathlib).
finding.suggested_fix: Delete line 25: `from pathlib import Path`. No other pathlib names are imported, so the entire line can be removed.
